segments.text: All right, so yeah, I'm writing a profile of Levy. Uh, she's one of the other interns. I understand you guys have worked together in the past.
Yeah. Yeah, so she's our communications intern. Uh, this is her last week, and we're really gonna miss her. And I'm the Communications Specialist here at eScience.
Awesome. Uh, can you tell me a little bit more about the projects you've worked on or that you've gotten to see her work on?
Yeah. So, Levy was here for a number of things. Um, kind of the first thing she was hired on to do, uh, was to help with the formatting, uh, and the written content of our newsletter.
Mm-hmm.
We have a weekly newsletter or bulletin that we send out, uh, that's largely about data science and AI content coming out of the eScience Institute.
Mm-hmm.

Um, Levy was such a perfect and unique fit for this, because I, I think it's very hard sometimes to find someone who likes writing, knows how to do it, knows how to do a lot of communications and media tasks, and is also, like, someone who knows what data science is and is actually-
Mm-hmm.

... very proficient in it. So, she was a really strong fit for that. Um, some of the projects that we've done since then, uh, have been a few things. We launched the very first Instagram for our institute. Uh, and she's made several really beautiful posts on Canva for that. Uh, she's also been very involved in updating our website. We have a WordPress-based website.
Mm-hmm.
And it is, don't quote me on this, a pain in the butt.
(laughs)
Um, and she's really, really good, um, at going in and figuring out-
Mm-hmm.
... this little quirk that nobody else can quite figure out and making the website look exactly how it needs to.
That's awesome.
So that's a few examples of some things we've been working on together.
And how long has she been working with your team?
Uh, Levy has been... You know, that's a better (laughs) question for my boss, Sarah-
Okay.
... because I started this job in January, and Levy had been here before me.
Okay.
So, for a while. You'd have to get-
Yeah.
... the exact date from her.

I, I can text her too, yeah. Um, uh, yeah, that's awesome. Uh, what would you say... Uh, well, okay, so she was in this job before you. Uh, have there been any specific projects that really stood out to you as, that, you know, as something really cool she's worked on whilst she's been with your team?

Yeah, that's a good question. So in the five, six months that I've been here and gotten to experience Levy, I think that, um, some things that really stood out were, uh, you know, she made, um, these highlights for our Instagram-
Mm-hmm.
... that look really sharp. Um, she helped our Instagram have this really cohesive look. Um, so basically it's not just like regular highlights on Instagram.
Mm-hmm.
It's, you know, the cover for the highlights that has a similar graphic design-
Mm-hmm.
... if that makes sense. So she offered a lot of, like, really, um, strong design sensibilities, uh, for the launch of our Instagram. She also was really involved with a lot of social media metrics-
Mm-hmm.

... and we found out pretty quickly how to track them, the best platforms for tracking them. Uh, so I think that she's really great at, you know, a lot of graphic design capabilities as well as, um, what's the word I'm looking for? Where it's, um, a technical review I think-
Mm-hmm.
... is what some people call it. If we need to compare platforms, if we need to find out different free options-
Mm-hmm.

... uh, to improve storage on our website, to collect social media metrics, Levy's definitely the person to do it. She's very thorough. She's really good, uh, at comparing different platforms out there and figuring out what the best option is and whether or not something's worth paying for.
Yeah.
She-

For sure. All right, um, uh, what would you say you, has been her most valuable contribution to your team? You've covered it a little bit, but, you know, over the course of the months you've worked together.
Yeah. So, um, I think that one of the most valuable contributions she makes is she does our newsletter every week. So-
Mm-hmm.
... I write most of the content, but she's the one formatting it in Mailchimp. And that can be such a pain in the butt for me and my boss, right?
Mm-hmm.
I think that it's one of those tasks nobody really wants to do, and Levy's like the queen of grunt work. She's so good at it. (laughs)
Mm-hmm.

Uh, and she, you know, can work in that platform very quickly. Um, so that's been really valuable 'cause that saves everyone else a lot of time. And then our newsletter ends up looking really strong. Um, so I would say that's probably one of the things she does pretty much every week that's been consistently really great. Um, she also, uh, was here during a lull in my position.
Mm-hmm.

So there was a gap between when the last person in my position left and when they were able to hire me. And so Levy was here doing a lot of things while we were understaffed in our communications role.
Mm-hmm.

Uh, really making sure that everything stayed together. Uh, 'cause, you know, without my role here, it's very hard to maintain the newsletter. It's very hard to maintain press releases, any kind of social media, the website.
Yeah.
So she's been kind of holding the fort down. Um, and I came to this job, and she, you know, was somebody who really partnered with me to both maintain and improve what we have-
Mm-hmm.
... but also to create a lot of new channels and platforms.
Yeah.
Uh, we also got up on Bluesky while she's here.
Yeah.
Um, so yeah, I think that Levy is someone who's brought a lot to the team, especially at a time where there was a lot of catching up to do.

Mm-hmm. Yeah. For sure. All right. Well, thank you. Uh, we, uh, this is very short. I'm just, you know, looking for references. Uh, is there anything else I should know about Levy before I, uh, head out?
She's so fun to work with.
Mm-hmm.

Uh, I, I think she's just... She's got a lovely personality. Um, she is always traveling, so she always has, like, kind of some fun stories from wherever she went on the weekend in the domestic US. She's always like, "I went to San Diego. Oh, I went to New York this weekend." Uh, she's a really good photographer, actually.
Oh, yeah.
I, I would quote me on that. She has this, um... She did a bunch of photography for us. I should have talked about this, 'cause this was actually one of my favorite things.
Yeah, please.
Um, she... We needed some photos for our annual report, uh, and our photo database is really outdated. Uh, we needed some more recent photos. We needed some photos of people working.
Mm-hmm.
And photos of people who have signed media release forms.
Mm-hmm.

Uh, and Levy really revolutionized the game on that one. She's got a great eye. Um, you should even just see the wallpaper on her laptop. It looks like an award-winning photo, and she just took it. It's like of a subway in New York, like moving.
Mm-hmm.
But there, there's like a piece of it that's st- She's great.
Yeah.
So I think she's a really talented photographer and media professional in that way. Um, and that has been such an asset coming into this job.
Mm-hmm.
Because it's, it's just amazing to have that support.

Mm-hmm. Well, thank you so much. That's exactly what I, I need to hear. Um, I'll let you get back to it. Uh, please stay in touch if there's anything else. This is just... It's a very short piece. I just needed to, you know, talk to people who know her work. Um, and I really appreciate you taking time to do it.
Yeah, of course. Um, if you don't mind, anything you direct quote me on, can you just, like, email it to me?
Yeah, I can do that.
Thank you. I just want to make sure I don't say anything with my new boss that's like, "And we're short staffed."
Yeah.
Because I don't want to give that impression of our team.
Yeah. Understood. Um, thank you. Uh, stay in touch, and I will email you the quotes when I have it done, probably in the next 48 hours or so.
Awesome. Thanks, Declan.
All right. Thanks again.
I appreciate it.
Have a good day. Bye.
You too. Bye.
